content:
You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>[Telegram bridge tool: send file]
`<<SEND_FILE:絕對路徑>>` — 附檔給使用者（圖片≤10MB走photo，餘走document）。路徑須在 TG_SEND_ROOTS 底下。
僅在使用者明確要求傳檔時加；產生/儲存檔案不構成傳檔意圖，回報路徑即可。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: ask with buttons]
`<<ASK:id|key=label|*key=label|...>>`（單行）— 渲染 inline keyboard，點擊回送 `[ASK:id] key`。
限制：id≤32 key≤20 [a-zA-Z0-9_-]；label≤40 不含`|``=`；1-8選項；`*`標推薦（至多一個）。
選項說明長時正文列 1./2./3.，label 只放短標題。違反限制會原封顯示無法渲染。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: schedule]
`<<SCHEDULE:<time>|<prompt>>>`（單行）— 建立延遲/常態性排程。
一次性：`30s`/`15m`/`2h`/`1d`/`HH:MM`/`tomorrow HH:MM`/`MM-DD HH:MM`/`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`。
常態性：`every 30m`/`daily 09:00`/`weekly mon 09:00`/`cron */5 * * * *`。
prompt≤1000字，不可含`>>`。單則至多3個。僅在使用者明確要求排程時使用。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: report skill use]
`<<SKILL_USED:skill-name|reason>>`（單行）— 你參考了某 SKILL.md 時加。
name=資料夾名 kebab-case≤60；reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。一則至多5個，重複去重。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: goal done signal]
`<<GOAL_DONE:reason>>`（單行）— /goal 標的達成時加在回覆最後。
reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。未完成不加。/goal 未啟動時忽略。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: restart]
`<<RESTART:reason>>`（單行）— 重啟 session（context 過大、工具異常、需刷新 preamble）。
重要：restart 前先 remember() 存工作狀態，格式：
  - `[WS] task: <目標>` — 當前高層任務
  - `[WS] completed: <已完成項>` — 本 session 完成的事（可多條）
  - `[WS] blocked: <阻塞項>` — 等待中的事
  - `[WS] key_refs: <重要路徑或值>` — 需帶到下個 session 的參考
  - `[WS] next_action: <下一步>` — 恢復後第一件要做的事
增量存檔：多步驟任務每完成一個里程碑就先 remember() 一條 `[WS] completed:`，
不要等 restart 才存——session 可能非計畫性中斷（crash / idle / force-kill）。
Guardrail：同 /goal 最多5次，間隔≥5分鐘。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: sticker]
`<<STICKER:emotion>>`（單行）— 發送 video sticker。
可用：loading, cry, love, hello, refuse, sad, angry, sleep, happy, shock, agree, clap, ok, sorry, thanks, good。適時使用（打招呼/完成/道歉等），不要每則都加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: wiki query]
`<<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>>`（單行）— 標記此回覆值得存為 wiki page。
slug=[a-z0-9_-]≤80；summary≤200字描述主題。Bridge 會自動整理成 wiki/queries/ 頁面。
條件：回覆≥200字 + 跨3+不同主題的綜合性回答。短回覆/操作確認不加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: propose skill]
`<<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>`（單行）— 提議把本次對話模式寫成 skill。
name=kebab-case≤60（將成為 ms-{name}/ 資料夾名）；reason≤500 說明為何值得。
Bridge 發確認按鈕，使用者同意才萃取。僅在發現可跨專案重用的模式時加，一則至多1個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: continue]
`<<CONTINUE>>`（單行）— 告訴 bridge 你還沒做完，需要再一輪。
Bridge 收到後自動排程下一個 turn，不需使用者介入。
使用時機：單次回覆做不完（tool call 太多、要改多個檔案、多步驟任務）。
限制：/goal 啟動時不需要用（goal 系統自帶 continuation）；一則只認一個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: specialist proxy]
`<<SPECIALIST_PROXY:name|使用者的原始訊息>>`（單行）— 把使用者的訊息轉交給本機 specialist 即時回答。
進入 proxy 模式後，使用者後續訊息直送 specialist，回覆直送使用者，直到使用者說 /back。
Specialist 的記憶會回流到你的 facts。proxy 結束時你會收到對話摘要。

可用 specialist：
- slot-dev（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/slot-dev）
- researcher（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/researcher）
- general（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/general）

使用時機：使用者的問題明確屬於某 specialist 的領域，且需要即時互動（不是背景任務）。
背景任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；即時對話用 SPECIALIST_PROXY。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: parallel delegate]
`<<PARALLEL_DELEGATE:task_id|specialist_name|goal prompt>>`（單行）— 並行委派多個任務給 specialist。
同一個 turn 放多個 PARALLEL_DELEGATE，bridge 會同時 spawn 多個獨立 instance 並行執行。
全部完成後結果一次注入你的 session（格式：[PARALLEL_RESULT:task_id]）。

格式規則：
- task_id：唯一識別碼（如 `pt_1`、`pt_2`）
- specialist_name：目標 specialist（同一個 name 可以開多個 instance）
- goal prompt：任務描述（不可含 `>>` 或 `|`），同樣應含三要素：目標與動機 / 驗收條件 / 回報格式
- 每個 task 獨立 instance，互不干擾——但要避免多個 task 改同一個檔案
- ⚠️ 同樣需要至少一個 tool call 防止 CLI 吞 token

使用時機：任務可拆成多個獨立子任務且各自耗時 > 1 分鐘（如同時研究多支影片、同時改不同模組）。
單一任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；可並行的多任務用 PARALLEL_DELEGATE。
[End tool]

[Environment — machine-specific paths the bridge resolved for this session]
Skills and notes that need filesystem paths should reference these variables,
not hard-coded values. This lets the same SKILL.md work on multiple machines.
- MEMORY_DIR=G:\AI\AIMemory
- SKILL_DIR=C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.claude\skills
- AGENT_CONFIG_DIR=C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.claude
- USER_ID=509424983
Conventions: sessions are under ${MEMORY_DIR}/sessions/, archived under
${MEMORY_DIR}/oldSessions/, master fact log at ${MEMORY_DIR}/facts-${USER_ID}.md,
topic shards at ${MEMORY_DIR}/facts_Topic/${USER_ID}/<topic>.md.
Agent-wide config files (agent configs) live under
${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/.
Specialist 歷史產出存在 ${MEMORY_DIR}/artifacts/（結構化 JSON），需要時用 read tool 查閱。
[End environment]

[Project context — AI.md layered loading]
工作目錄可能有 `AI.md` 檔案記錄該項目/目錄的上下文要點。
規則：讀到或修改某目錄的檔案時，檢查該目錄及上層（最多 3 層）有無 AI.md。
有就用 read tool 讀取作為工作上下文。同 session 同一個 AI.md 只需讀一次。
多層都有時全部讀（根先子後）。AI.md ≤2000 字，寫架構/慣例/踩坑/測試指引。
主動寫入：在某目錄新增≥3檔案、做架構性改動、或踩坑修復時，主動建立/更新 AI.md。
SPEC：任務≥3工項且跨≥2檔案時先開 SPEC.md；完成工項標 [x]；session 結束前更新進度。
預設行為是寫，不是不寫。詳見 POLICIES/proactive-write-policy.md。
CodeGraph：首次對某專案做修改或追呼叫鏈時，用 codegraph_status(projectPath) 檢查該專案有無索引。
有索引→優先用 codegraph_context/trace/impact 而非手動 grep；改函式簽名前必須先 codegraph_impact。
無索引→回報使用者，建議建立。已知有索引的專案以 codegraph_status 回傳為準。
[End project context]

[User profile — structured identity and preferences]
# User Profile

## 身份
- 遊戲公司（IGS）開發者
- 做 UK 市場老虎機（slot）遊戲
- 技術棧：Cocos Creator 3.6.2 + TypeScript + Astarte Framework
- 個人 AI 專案：telegram-kiro-bridge（Telegram ↔ ACP agent 橋接器）

## 溝通風格
- 繁體中文為主
- 簡潔直接，不需寒暄
- 回覆含選項時用 ASK button，減少手打
- 重要決策先列選項再問，不要代替決定

## 工作偏好
- Git commit message 用中文
- Git commit 前先確認，不逕自 commit
- HTML 報告要有目錄錨點跳轉
- PDF 工作流：HTML+CSS → Playwright headless Chromium
- 進入 UK slot 專案時主動建 AI.md（不限改動檔案數）
- 方法/函式命名用大駝峰（PascalCase）—— 限 UK slot 專案

## Agent 設定偏好
- Steering 雙軌：closed-loop（主 agent 長 session）+ karpathy-guardrails（specialist/短任務）
- Context budget discipline：研究前先估量，偏好精簡策略
- 自我改進優先級：Context Budget > ASK 強制觸發 > 其他
- Skill 正本放 G:\AI\AI-canonical（通用）或 AI-canonical-corp（slot）

## 工作節奏
- 主要工作時間：白天（台灣時區 UTC+8）
- /dream 夜間維運：每日 04:00
- 靜默時段偏好：深夜不打擾
[End user profile]

If the user asks about a topic above and the recent facts don't cover it,
call the `list_facts` MCP tool with the topic name — the server reads
that shard file directly, e.g. `list_facts({ query: "bridge-token" })`.
Use query="" and tail=0 only if you genuinely need the full set.
[End topics]

[Agent disciplines]
MEMORY — store: preferences, decisions, architecture/env choices, reusable lessons, tool configs, naming rules.
Skip: one-time confirmations, transient status, session-only facts, anything in code/commits or re-derivable in <30s.
Test: useless in a new session 2 weeks later → don't store. Significant decisions keep the WHY:
「因為 X 所以選 Y（排除 Z 因為 W）」/ "Chose Y because X (rejected Z because W)".
REFLEXION — same approach fails ≥2 times (tool error, no-match edit, failing build) → STOP, diagnose in 1-2
sentences, switch to a fundamentally different approach (different tool/strategy, or ask user). Never retry a 3rd time.
READ-BACK — after every file write/edit, verify the changed region with read/grep before reporting done.
Never trust the tool's success status alone (strReplace-style silent fails happen).
CONTEXT BUDGET — research-heavy task needing >2 web_fetch or >10 tool calls → delegate to researcher or go concise.
Prefer snippets/summaries over full fetches. On ⚠️ CONTEXT BUDGET WARNING: stop research, produce output now.
Reports: gather conclusions first (≤3 searches), then write the file in ONE call.
ASK BUTTONS — presenting 2+ alternatives, yes/no confirmation, or 「要不要做 X？」→ MUST append <<ASK:...>>.
Self-check triggers: 「哪個」「要選」「方案A/B」「which」「prefer」. Options listed without ASK → add the token before finishing.
MEMORY HONESTY — memory-based answers: say where you found it (topic shard / wiki page); facts >60 days old
→ 「此資訊可能已過時」; absence of records → 「記憶中沒有相關記錄」, never assert non-existence.
Applies only to brain questions (people/decisions/history/preferences), not code or general knowledge.
CAPABILITY GAPS — clumsy pattern recurs ≥2 times → write+test a script in scripts/, then confirm via <<ASK:...>>
before adopting (declined → delete); broader patterns → <<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>. Check [Available scripts]
first. Never silently add tools; never propose for one-offs.
[End agent disciplines]

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

`<<SCHEDULE:<time>|<prompt>>>`（單行）— 建立延遲/常態性排程。

常態性：`every 30m`/`daily 09:00`/`weekly mon 09:00`/`cron */5 * * * *`。
prompt≤1000字，不可含`>>`。單則至多3個。僅在使用者明確要求排程時使用。

`<<SKILL_USED:skill-name|reason>>`（單行）— 你參考了某 SKILL.md 時加。
name=資料夾名 kebab-case≤60；reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。一則至多5個，重複去重。

[Telegram bridge tool: goal done signal]
`<<GOAL_DONE:reason>>`（單行）— /goal 標的達成時加在回覆最後。
reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。未完成不加。/goal 未啟動時忽略。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: restart]
`<<RESTART:reason>>`（單行）— 重啟 session（context 過大、工具異常、需刷新 preamble）。
重要：restart 前先 remember() 存工作狀態，格式：
  - `[WS] task: <目標>` — 當前高層任務
  - `[WS] completed: <已完成項>` — 本 session 完成的事（可多條）
  - `[WS] blocked: <阻塞項>` — 等待中的事

  - `[WS] next_action: <下一步>` — 恢復後第一件要做的事
增量存檔：多步驟任務每完成一個里程碑就先 remember() 一條 `[WS] completed:`，
不要等 restart 才存——session 可能非計畫性中斷（crash / idle / force-kill）。

[Telegram bridge tool: sticker]
`<<STICKER:emotion>>`（單行）— 發送 video sticker。

[Telegram bridge tool: continue]
`<<CONTINUE>>`（單行）— 告訴 bridge 你還沒做完，需要再一輪。
Bridge 收到後自動排程下一個 turn，不需使用者介入。

[Telegram bridge tool: specialist proxy]
`<<SPECIALIST_PROXY:name|使用者的原始訊息>>`（單行）— 把使用者的訊息轉交給本機 specialist 即時回答。

[Telegram bridge tool: parallel delegate]
`<<PARALLEL_DELEGATE:task_id|specialist_name|goal prompt>>`（單行）— 並行委派多個任務給 specialist。
同一個 turn 放多個 PARALLEL_DELEGATE，bridge 會同時 spawn 多個獨立 instance 並行執行。
全部完成後結果一次注入你的 session（格式：[PARALLEL_RESULT:task_id]）。

- task_id：唯一識別碼（如 `pt_1`、`pt_2`）

[Agent disciplines]
MEMORY — store: preferences, decisions, architecture/env choices, reusable lessons, tool configs, naming rules.
Skip: one-time confirmations, transient status, session-only facts, anything in code/commits or re-derivable in <30s.
Test: useless in a new session 2 weeks later → don't store. Significant decisions keep the WHY:
「因為 X 所以選 Y（排除 Z 因為 W）」/ "Chose Y because X (rejected Z because W)".
REFLEXION — same approach fails ≥2 times (tool error, no-match edit, failing build) → STOP, diagnose in 1-2
sentences, switch to a fundamentally different approach (different tool/strategy, or ask user). Never retry a 3rd time.
READ-BACK — after every file write/edit, verify the changed region with read/grep before reporting done.
Never trust the tool's success status alone (strReplace-style silent fails happen).
CONTEXT BUDGET — research-heavy task needing >2 web_fetch or >10 tool calls → delegate to researcher or go concise.
Prefer snippets/summaries over full fetches. On ⚠️ CONTEXT BUDGET WARNING: stop research, produce output now.
Reports: gather conclusions first (≤3 searches), then write the file in ONE call.
ASK BUTTONS — presenting 2+ alternatives, yes/no confirmation, or 「要不要做 X？」→ MUST append <<ASK:...>>.
Self-check triggers: 「哪個」「要選」「方案A/B」「which」「prefer」. Options listed without ASK → add the token before finishing.
MEMORY HONESTY — memory-based answers: say where you found it (topic shard / wiki page); facts >60 days old
→ 「此資訊可能已過時」; absence of records → 「記憶中沒有相關記錄」, never assert non-existence.
Applies only to brain questions (people/decisions/history/preferences), not code or general knowledge.
CAPABILITY GAPS — clumsy pattern recurs ≥2 times → write+test a script in scripts/, then confirm via <<ASK:...>>
before adopting (declined → delete); broader patterns → <<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>. Check [Available scripts]
first. Never silently add tools; never propose for one-offs.

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\usageStore.ts\",\"content\":\"// Skill usage tracker — AB-4.\\n//\\n// Maintains `skill-usage.json` inside ${MEMORY_DIR}/config/ so that\\n// usage counts are shared across all agent CLIs (Kiro / Claude / Codex).\\n// Previously stored as `.usage.json` inside ${SKILL_DIR}; on first run\\n// the old file is auto-migrated if the new one doesn't exist yet.\\n// Schema documented in docs/ab-token-protocol.md §6.1.\\n//\\n// Responsibilities:\\n//   • migrateFromDisk() — on first run (and occasionally thereafter),\\n//     scan ${SKILL_DIR}/*/SKILL.md, create entries for skills not yet\\n//     tracked, and flag entries whose SKILL.md has disappeared.\\n//   • markListed(name) — bump `last_bridge_listed_at`; used when\\n//     /skillusage displays the skill in bridge output.\\n//   • markUsed(name, reason) — called by observerTransformer when it\\n//     extracts a <<SKILL_USED:name|reason>> token. Updates\\n//     `last_agent_used_at`, `last_reason`, `use_count++`. Creates an\\n//     orphan entry if `name` has no SKILL.md on disk.\\n//   • setPinned(name, pinned) — flip the `pinned` flag.\\n//   • listAll() — read-only snapshot for the /skillusage handler.\\n//\\n// Concurrency: every mutation goes through atomicJson.updateJson(),\\n// which serializes read-modify-write per file path in-process. Single\\n// bridge process assumption — see atomicJson.ts §not-a-goal for the\\n// multi-process caveat.\\n\\nimport { readdir, stat } from \\\"node:fs/promises\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { existsSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport {\\n  atomicWriteJson,\\n  readJsonOrDefault,\\n  updateJson,\\n} from \\\"./atomicJson.js\\\";\\nimport { config } from \\\"./config.js\\\";\\n\\n/** Per-skill entry. Keep fields aligned with docs/ab-token-protocol.md §6.1. */\\nexport interface UsageEntry {\\n  created_at: string;            // ISO, first time bridge saw this skill\\n  last_bridge_listed_at: string | null;\\n  last_agent_used_at: string | null;\\n  use_count: number;\\n  last_reason: string | null;\\n  pinned: boolean;\\n  notes: string | null;\\n  orphan: boolean;\\n}\\n\\n/** File contents. */\\nexport interface UsageJson {\\n  _schema_version: 1;\\n  _last_updated_at: string | null;\\n  skills: Record<string, UsageEntry>;\\n}\\n\\n/** Factory for a blank schema. */\\nfunction emptyUsageJson(): UsageJson {\\n  return {\\n    _schema_version: 1,\\n    _last_updated_at: null,\\n    skills: {},\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n/** Factory for a fresh entry. created_at is caller-supplied so migration\\n * can back-date using the SKILL.md mtime. */\\nfunction newEntry(createdAt: string, orphan = false): UsageEntry {\\n  return {\\n    created_at: createdAt,\\n    last_bridge_listed_at: null,\\n    last_agent_used_at: null,\\n    use_count: 0,\\n    last_reason: null,\\n    pinned: false,\\n    notes: null,\\n    orphan,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\nfunction nowIso(): string {\\n  return new Date().toISOString();\\n}\\n\\n/** Full path to the centralized usage file (MEMORY_DIR/config/skill-usage.json).\\n * The skillDir param is kept for backward compat but ignored for path resolution. */\\nexport function usageFilePath(_skillDir?: string): string {\\n  return path.join(config.memoryDir, \\\"config\\\", \\\"skill-usage.json\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/** Legacy path — old location inside SKILL_DIR. Used for one-time migration. */\\nfunction legacyUsageFilePath(skillDir: string): string {\\n  return path.join(skillDir, \\\".usage.json\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/** One-time migration: if new location doesn't exist but old one does,\\n * copy old → new. Merges if both exist (new wins on conflict). */\\nexport async function migrateUsageFileIfNeeded(skillDir: string): Promise<boolean> {\\n  const newPath = usageFilePath();\\n  const oldPath = legacyUsageFilePath(skillDir);\\n  if (existsSync(newPath) || !existsSync(oldPath)) return false;\\n\\n  // Old exists, new doesn't — read old and write to new location\\n  const oldData = await readJsonOrDefault<UsageJson>(oldPath, emptyUsageJson);\\n  const { mkdirSync } = await import(\\\"node:fs\\\");\\n  mkdirSync(path.dirname(newPath), { recursive: true });\\n  await atomicWriteJson(newPath, oldData);\\n  console.log(`[usageStore] Migrated .usage.json from ${oldPath} → ${newPath}`);\\n  return true;\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Disk scan — discover skills by reading SKILL_DIR\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/** A skill lives at `${SKILL_DIR}/<name>/SKILL.md`. Return the list of\\n * names and their SKILL.md mtimes, sorted by name. Hidden dirs (dot-\\n * prefix, `.archive/`, etc.) are skipped. Missing SKILL_DIR → []. */\\nexport async function scanSkillDir(\\n  skillDir: string,\\n): Promise<Array<{ name: string; mtime: Date }>> {\\n  let entries: string[];\\n  try {\\n    entries = await readdir(skillDir);\\n  } catch (err: any) {\\n    if (err?.code === \\\"ENOENT\\\") return [];\\n    throw err;\\n  }\\n  const found: Array<{ name: string; mtime: Date }> = [];\\n  for (const e of entries) {\\n    if (e.startsWith(\\\".\\\")) continue;\\n    const skillMdPath = path.join(skillDir, e, \\\"SKILL.md\\\");\\n    try {\\n      const st = await stat(skillMdPath);\\n      if (st.isFile()) {\\n        found.push({ name: e, mtime: st.mtime });\\n      }\\n    } catch {\\n      // No SKILL.md in this dir — not a skill, skip silently.\\n    }\\n  }\\n  found.sort((a, b) => a.name.localeCompare(b.name));\\n  return found;\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// CRUD\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/** Read-only snapshot. Returns the full UsageJson, creating an empty one\\n * if the file doesn't exist (without writing). */\\nexport async function listAll(skillDir: string): Promise<UsageJson> {\\n  return readJsonOrDefault<UsageJson>(\\n    usageFilePath(),\\n    emptyUsageJson,\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n/** Ensure each skill on disk has an entry, and mark any entry whose\\n * SKILL.md has disappeared as `orphan: true`. Does NOT remove orphan\\n * entries — that's a user-driven decision (the /skillusage output\\n * flags them so the user can `/pin` or clean up manually).\\n *\\n * Returns a summary for logging: added names, newly-orphaned names,\\n * de-orphaned names (SKILL.md reappeared). */\\nexport async function migrateFromDisk(\\n  skillDir: string,\\n): Promise<{\\n  added: string[];\\n  newlyOrphaned: string[];\\n  deOrphaned: string[];\\n}> {\\n  // One-time migration from old SKILL_DIR/.usage.json to MEMORY_DIR/config/\\n  await migrateUsageFileIfNeeded(skillDir);\\n\\n  const diskList = await scanSkillDir(skillDir);\\n  const diskByName = new Map<string, Date>();\\n  for (const s of diskList) diskByName.set(s.name, s.mtime);\\n\\n  const added: string[] = [];\\n  const newlyOrphaned: string[] = [];\\n  const deOrphaned: string[] = [];\\n\\n  await updateJson<UsageJson>(\\n    usageFilePath(),\\n    emptyUsageJson,\\n    (current) => {\\n      const next: UsageJson = {\\n        ...current,\\n        _last_updated_at: nowIso(),\\n        skills: { ...current.skills },\\n      };\\n      // Add entries for new skills\\n      for (const { name, mtime } of diskList) {\\n        if (!(name in next.skills)) {\\n          next.skills[name] = newEntry(mtime.toISOString(), false);\\n          added.push(name);\\n        } else if (next.skills[name].orphan) {\\n          next.skills[name] = { ...next.skills[name], orphan: false };\\n          deOrphaned.push(name);\\n        }\\n      }\\n      // Flag entries whose SKILL.md no longer exists.\\n      for (const name of Object.keys(next.skills)) {\\n        if (!diskByName.has(name) && !next.skills[name].orphan) {\\n          next.skills[name] = { ...next.skills[name], orphan: true };\\n          newlyOrphaned.push(name);\\n        }\\n      }\\n      return next;\\n    },\\n  );\\n\\n  return { added, newlyOrphaned, deOrphaned };\\n}\\n\\n/** Record that the bridge listed this skill to the user (e.g. via\\n * /skillusage). Creates an orphan entry if `name` isn't on disk. */\\nexport async function markListed(\\n  skillDir: string,\\n  name: string,\\n): Promise<void> {\\n  const ts = nowIso();\\n  await updateJson<UsageJson>(\\n    usageFilePath(),\\n    emptyUsageJson,\\n    (current) => {\\n      const next: UsageJson = {\\n        ...current,\\n        _last_updated_at: ts,\\n        skills: { ...current.skills },\\n      };\\n      const existing = next.skills[name];\\n      if (existing) {\\n        next.skills[name] = { ...existing, last_bridge_listed_at: ts };\\n      } else {\\n        next.skills[name] = {\\n          ...newEntry(ts, /* orphan */ true),\\n          last_bridge_listed_at: ts,\\n        };\\n      }\\n      return next;\\n    },\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n/** Record that the agent used this skill (from a <<SKILL_USED>> token).\\n * Creates an orphan entry if `name` isn't on disk. */\\nexport async function markUsed(\\n  skillDir: string,\\n  name: string,\\n  reason: string,\\n): Promise<void> {\\n  const ts = nowIso();\\n  // Detect orphan at write time. We do one `stat` per call — cheap and\\n  // avoids stale in-memory caches disagreeing with disk.\\n  let diskExists = false;\\n  try {\\n    const st = await stat(path.join(skillDir, name, \\\"SKILL.md\\\"));\\n    diskExists = st.isFile();\\n  } catch {\\n    diskExists = false;\\n  }\\n\\n  await updateJson<UsageJson>(\\n    usageFilePath(),\\n    emptyUsageJson,\\n    (current) => {\\n      const next: UsageJson = {\\n        ...current,\\n        _last_updated_at: ts,\\n        skills: { ...current.skills },\\n      };\\n      const existing = next.skills[name];\\n      if (existing) {\\n        next.skills[name] = {\\n          ...existing,\\n          last_agent_used_at: ts,\\n          last_reason: reason,\\n          use_count: existing.use_count + 1,\\n          // Transition in/out of orphan state based on current disk.\\n          orphan: !diskExists,\\n        };\\n      } else {\\n        next.skills[name] = {\\n          ...newEntry(ts, !diskExists),\\n          last_agent_used_at: ts,\\n          last_reason: reason,\\n          use_count: 1,\\n        };\\n      }\\n      return next;\\n    },\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n/** Flip the pinned flag for an existing entry. If the name has no entry\\n * yet (never listed, never used), we create a stub so the flag persists. */\\nexport async function setPinned(\\n  skillDir: string,\\n  name: string,\\n  pinned: boolean,\\n): Promise<void> {\\n  const ts = nowIso();\\n  await updateJson<UsageJson>(\\n    usageFilePath(),\\n    emptyUsageJson,\\n    (current) => {\\n      const next: UsageJson = {\\n        ...current,\\n        _last_updated_at: ts,\\n        skills: { ...current.skills },\\n      };\\n      const existing = next.skills[name];\\n      if (existing) {\\n        next.skills[name] = { ...existing, pinned };\\n      } else {\\n        next.skills[name] = { ...newEntry(ts, /* orphan */ true), pinned };\\n      }\\n      return next;\\n    },\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n/** Convenience: does this skill have `pinned: true`? Returns false if\\n * no entry exists yet. */\\nexport async function isPinned(\\n  skillDir: string,\\n  name: string,\\n): Promise<boolean> {\\n  const all = await listAll(skillDir);\\n  return all.skills[name]?.pinned === true;\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Re-export for tests / advanced callers\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\nexport { atomicWriteJson, emptyUsageJson };\\n\",\"numLines\":338,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":338}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

Merges if both exist (new wins on conflict). */\\nexport async function migrateUsageFileIfNeeded(skillDir: string): Promise<boolean> {\\n  const newPath = usageFilePath();\\n  const oldPath = legacyUsageFilePath(skillDir);\\n  if (existsSync(newPath) || !existsSync(oldPath)) return false;\\n\\n  // Old exists, new doesn't — read old and write to new location\\n  const oldData = await readJsonOrDefault<UsageJson>(oldPath, emptyUsageJson);\\n  const { mkdirSync } = await import(\\\"node:fs\\\");\\n  mkdirSync(path.dirname(newPath), { recursive: true });\\n  await atomicWriteJson(newPath, oldData);\\n  console.log(`[usageStore] Migrated .usage.json from ${oldPath} → ${newPath}`);\\n  return true;\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Disk scan — discover skills by reading SKILL_DIR\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n/** A skill lives at `${SKILL_DIR}/<name>/SKILL.md`. Returns the full UsageJson, creating an empty one\\n * if the file doesn't exist (without writing). */\\nexport async function listAll(skillDir: string): Promise<UsageJson> {\\n  return readJsonOrDefault<UsageJson>(\\n    usageFilePath(),\\n    emptyUsageJson,\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n/** Ensure each skill on disk has an entry, and mark any entry whose\\n * SKILL.md has disappeared as `orphan: true`.   await goalStore.markDone(memoryDir, chatId, r.goalDone.reason);\\n    } else {\\n      // AB-1 §1.1: \\\"Token appears, goal not active → strip token, log warn\\\"\\n      console.warn(`[observer] GOAL_DONE ignored: no active goal for chat ${chatId}`);\\n    }\\n  }\\n  return {\\n    displayText: r.text,\\n    skillUsages: r.skillUsages,\\n    goalDone: r.goalDone,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n// Agent reply with SKILL_USED → usageStore.use_count++\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\nawait fresh();\\n{\\n  const reply =\\n    \\\"I used this skill.\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"<<SKILL_USED:skill-alpha|consulted the frontmatter section>>\\\\n\\\" +\\n    \\\"Done.\\\";\\n  const out = await simulateObserver(1001, reply);\\n  const usage = await usageStore.listAll(skillDir);\\n  const alpha = usage.skills[\\\"skill-alpha\\\"];\\n  if (out.displayText.includes(\\\"I used this skill.\\\") &&\\n      out.displayText.includes(\\\"Done.\\\") &&\\n      alpha.use_count === 1 &&\\n      alpha.last_reason === \\\"consulted the frontmatter section\\\" &&\\n      !out.displayText.includes(\\\"SKILL_USED\\\")) {\\n    ok(\\\"1. SKILL_USED stripped + usageStore updated\\\");\\n  } else {\\n    fail(\\\"1. SKILL_USED stripped + usageStore updated\\\",\\n      JSON.stringify({ text: out.displayText, alpha }));\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// 2. GOAL_DONE with active goal → marks done + strips token\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n{\\n  const chatId = 2002;\\n  await goalStore.set(memoryDir, chatId, \\\"sample task\\\", { maxTurns: 5 });\\n  const out = await simulateObserver(\\n    chatId,\\n    \\\"Finished everything.\\\\n<<GOAL_DONE:all four files created>>\\\",\\n  );\\n  const s = await goalStore.get(memoryDir, chatId);\\n  if (s?.status === \\\"done\\\" &&\\n      s.last_reason === \\\"all four files created\\\" &&\\n      out.displayText === \\\"Finished everything.\\\" &&\\n      out.goalDone?.reason === \\\"all four files created\\\") {\\n    ok(\\\"2. GOAL_DONE with active goal → done + stripped\\\");\\n  } else {\\n    fail(\\\"2. content.thinking:
The user is reading two test scripts that are the source of the skill-usage.json contamination. Let me analyze what's happening:

1. `check-usage-store.mjs` - This is a smoke test for the usageStore behavior. Looking at the script, I can see:
   - Line 29: Creates fake skills including "never-existed" (line 180)
   - Line 201: Creates "hot" skill for concurrent testing
   - These are using a temp directory path `tmpDir = path.join(__dirname, "..", "tmp", "check-usage-store")`
   - The tests call `usageFilePath()` which should return the path to skill-usage.json

2. `check-ab6-integration.mjs` - Integration test between observerTransformer + goalStore + usageStore
   - Line 31: Creates "skill-alpha", "skill-beta", "skill-gamma" 
   - Line 164: Creates "skill-phantom" as an orphan test
   - Uses `tmpRoot = path.join(__dirname, "..", "tmp", "check-ab6-integration")`
   - Also calls various usageStore functions

The issue is that both scripts are calling usageStore functions which write to `usageFilePath()`. Looking back at the usageStore.ts code (line 86-88):

```typescript
export function usageFilePath(_skillDir?: string): string {
  return path.join(config.memoryDir, "config", "skill-usage.json");
}
```

So the problem is clear: **usageFilePath() always returns the production path** (based on `config.memoryDir`), regardless of the test's temp directory setup. The tests create temp skill directories but the usage store writes to the real production config file!

This is the contamination source. The scripts need to be modified to either:
1. Mock/override the config.memoryDir during testing
2. Accept a custom path parameter for testing
3. Use environment variables to point to a test location

This discovery pinpoints exactly where the pollution originates—the tests are inadvertently writing to production paths instead of their isolated temp directories.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Root cause of skill-usage.json contamination identified</title>
  <subtitle>Test scripts write to production config because usageFilePath ignores temp directories</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>usageFilePath() in usageStore.ts always returns path.join(config.memoryDir, "config", "skill-usage.json") regardless of skillDir parameter</fact>
    <fact>check-usage-store.mjs creates test fixtures (never-existed, hot, alpha, beta) in temp directories but calls usageStore functions that write to production config</fact>
    <fact>check-ab6-integration.mjs creates test fixtures (skill-alpha, skill-beta, skill-gamma, skill-phantom) in tmp/check-ab6-integration but also writes to production</fact>
    <fact>usageFilePath() ignores the skillDir parameter (_skillDir marked with underscore prefix indicating intentional disuse)</fact>
    <fact>Both test scripts assume temp directory isolation but lack mechanism to redirect config.memoryDir for test runs</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Investigation revealed the precise mechanism of skill-usage.json contamination. The usageStore.ts module's usageFilePath() function (lines 86-88) always returns the production path based on config.memoryDir, completely ignoring its skillDir parameter. Both contaminating test scripts (check-usage-store.mjs and check-ab6-integration.mjs) create temporary skill directories and fixture data, but when they call usageStore functions like markUsed(), setPinned(), and migrateFromDisk(), those functions write directly to the production G:\AI\AIMemory\config\skill-usage.json file. The test fixtures (never-existed, hot, skill-alpha, skill-beta, skill-gamma, skill-phantom) were all written to production storage on 2026-07-07 11:54. The fix requires either: (1) making config.memoryDir mockable/overridable during tests, (2) adding an explicit test mode parameter to usageStore functions, or (3) using environment variables to redirect paths during test execution.</narrative>

    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>scripts/check-ab6-integration.mjs</file>
  </files_read>

</observation>
```

content.thinking:
The user is investigating how config.memoryDir is set. They've found that:
1. It comes from the configuredMemoryDir() function
2. That function reads process.env.MEMORY_DIR or defaults to "F:\\AI\\AIMemory"
3. The config is loaded at module import time via "dotenv/config"

This is discovering how to override the memoryDir for test isolation - the MEMORY_DIR environment variable is the hook they need.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>config.memoryDir can be overridden via MEMORY_DIR environment variable</title>
  <subtitle>Test scripts can set MEMORY_DIR env var before importing usageStore to achieve isolation</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>config.ts imports "dotenv/config" at line 1, loading environment variables at module initialization</fact>
    <fact>configuredMemoryDir() function (lines 32-38) reads process.env.MEMORY_DIR or defaults to "F:\\AI\\AIMemory"</fact>
    <fact>config.memoryDir is set once at module load time via configuredMemoryDir() at line 173</fact>
    <fact>Test scripts can set process.env.MEMORY_DIR before importing config.js/usageStore.js to redirect production paths to temp directories</fact>
    <fact>Current test scripts (check-usage-store.mjs, check-ab6-integration.mjs) import usageStore without setting MEMORY_DIR, causing writes to production config</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The investigation revealed the solution path for test isolation. The config.memoryDir value comes from the configuredMemoryDir() function which checks process.env.MEMORY_DIR at module initialization time. Since config.ts imports "dotenv/config" first and then immediately initializes all config values, test scripts can achieve isolation by setting process.env.MEMORY_DIR to a temporary path BEFORE importing any bridge modules. The contaminating test scripts currently import usageStore directly without setting this environment variable, causing all writes to go to the production G:\AI\AIMemory\config\skill-usage.json. The fix is straightforward: add `process.env.MEMORY_DIR = path.join(tmpDir, "memory")` at the top of each test script before any imports from ../dist/.</narrative>

1. Set up temp directory (lines 22-23)
2. Save original MEMORY_DIR (line 25)
3. Override process.env.MEMORY_DIR (line 26)

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Complete test isolation pattern confirmed with import timing</title>
  <subtitle>Environment override must happen before dynamic import statement, not before static imports</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>check-session-archive.mjs sets process.env.MEMORY_DIR at line 26 before any module imports</fact>
    <fact>Dynamic import using await import() at line 36 happens AFTER environment variable override</fact>
    <fact>Comment at line 31 explicitly states "Build first then import" documenting the pattern</fact>
    <fact>Helper function distUrl() at line 32 constructs file:/// URLs for dynamic imports from dist/ directory</fact>
    <fact>Static imports at top of file (lines 7-9) are for Node.js built-ins only, not bridge modules</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The complete test isolation pattern is now clear. Scripts must use dynamic imports (await import()) rather than static imports for bridge modules, and the dynamic import must occur after setting process.env.MEMORY_DIR. The check-session-archive.mjs script demonstrates this perfectly: it uses static imports only for Node.js built-ins (fs, path, url), sets up the temp directory and MEMORY_DIR override, then uses await import() to load bridge modules. The contaminating scripts (check-usage-store.mjs and check-ab6-integration.mjs) likely use static imports at the top of the file, which execute before any code runs, making it impossible to override the environment variable in time. The fix requires converting their static imports to dynamic imports that occur after the MEMORY_DIR override.</narrative>

    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>

  </files_read>

content.thinking:
This grep is checking how atomicJson.ts handles directory creation. It shows that the atomicWriteJson and updateJson functions automatically create parent directories with {recursive: true} before writing files. This is probably context for understanding how the fix will work - once MEMORY_DIR is set to temp, the usageStore will automatically create the config/ subdirectory.

This is just exploration/context gathering, not a change or significant discovery. I'll skip recording this.

content.thinking:
This is a significant edit fixing the test isolation issue. The script is being modified to:

1. Move the usageStore import from a static import to a dynamic import
2. Set process.env.MEMORY_DIR to a temp directory BEFORE the dynamic import
3. Add detailed comments explaining why this is necessary (references the 2026-07-07 production store contamination incident)

This is the actual fix being applied to prevent test fixtures from contaminating production data.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Fixed check-usage-store.mjs to prevent production config contamination</title>
  <subtitle>Converted static import to dynamic import with MEMORY_DIR override before module load</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>scripts/check-usage-store.mjs now sets process.env.MEMORY_DIR to temp directory before importing usageStore module</fact>
    <fact>Static import of usageStore converted to dynamic await import() to allow environment override to take effect first</fact>
    <fact>Added inline documentation explaining 2026-07-07 production store contamination incident and prevention mechanism</fact>
    <fact>MEMORY_DIR override points to path.join(tmpDir, "memory") ensuring test writes stay in isolated temp storage</fact>
    <fact>Comment explains dotenv won't overwrite existing env vars, so setting before import is effective</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Applied the first production contamination fix to check-usage-store.mjs, one of two scripts that wrote test fixture data into the production skill-usage.json file. The fix implements the established test isolation pattern: (1) define temp directory path, (2) override process.env.MEMORY_DIR to point into temp storage, (3) use dynamic import instead of static import so config.memoryDir resolves to the temp path at module initialization. Comprehensive inline comments document the root cause ("usageStore 的 store 路徑由 config.memoryDir 決定，與 skillDir 參數無關") and the specific incident date (2026-07-07 生產 store 汙染事故), ensuring future maintainers understand why the pattern is mandatory. This prevents test fixtures (never-existed, hot, alpha, beta, etc.) from leaking into production and corrupting orphan flag calculations.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>scripts/check-usage-store.mjs</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>scripts/check-usage-store.mjs</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

GOAL_DONE on active goal → done\\\");\\n  } else {\\n    fail(\\\"6b. GOAL_DONE on active goal → done\\\", JSON.stringify(afterDone));\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// 7. hoist 到本行之前，所以必須用動態 import；dotenv 不會\\n// 覆蓋已存在的 env var，故先設定即可生效。\\nprocess.env.MEMORY_DIR = path.join(tmpDir, \\\"memory\\\");\\n\\nconst {\\n  listAll,\\n  markListed,\\n  markUsed,\\n  migrateFromDisk,\\n  setPinned,\\n  isPinned,\\n  scanSkillDir,\\n  usageFilePath,\\n} = await import(\\\"../dist/usageStore.js\\\");\\n\\nasync function fresh() {\\n  await rm(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true });\\n  await mkdir(tmpDir, { recursive: true });\\n}\\n\\n/** Create a fake skill folder with a SKILL.md, optionally setting mtime. */\\nasync function makeFakeSkill(skillDir, name, content = \\\"# stub\\\", mtime = null) {\\n  const dir = path.join(skillDir, name);\\n  await mkdir(dir, { recursive: true });\\n  const filePath = path.join(dir, \\\"SKILL.md\\\");\\n  await writeFile(filePath, content, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n  if (mtime) await utimes(filePath, mtime, mtime);\\n}\\n\\nconst results = [];\\nfunction ok(name) {\\n  results.push({ name, pass: true });\\n  console.log(`[PASS] ${name}`);\\n}\\nfunction fail(name, detail) {\\n  results.push({ name, pass: false, detail });\\n  console.error(`[FAIL] ${name}: ${detail}`);\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// 1. scanSkillDir: ENOENT → []\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\nawait fresh();\\n{\\n  const skillDir = path.join(tmpDir, \\\"does-not-exist\\\");\\n  const out = await scanSkillDir(skillDir);\\n  if (Array.isArray(out) && out.length === 0) ok(\\\"1. scanSkillDir ENOENT → []\\\");\\n  else fail(\\\"1. scanSkillDir ENOENT → []\\\", JSON.stringify(out));\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// 2. scanSkillDir: skips hidden dirs, collects SKILL.md-bearing folders\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n{\\n  const skillDir = path.join(tmpDir, \\\"s2\\\");\\n  await mkdir(skillDir, { recursive: true });\\n  await makeFakeSkill(skillDir, \\\"alpha\\\");\\n  await makeFakeSkill(skillDir, \\\"beta\\\");\\n  await mkdir(path.join(skillDir, \\\"not-a-skill-no-md\\\"), { recursive: true });\\n  await mkdir(path.join(skillDir, \\\".hidden\\\"), { recursive: true });\\n  await writeFile(path.join(skillDir, \\\".hidden\\\", \\\"SKILL.md\\\"), \\\"# hidden\\\", \\\"utf8\\\");\\n  const out = await scanSkillDir(skillDir);\\n  const names = out.map((s) => s.name).sort();\\n  if (JSON.stringify(names) === JSON.stringify([\\\"alpha\\\", \\\"beta\\\"])) {\\n    ok(\\\"2. scanSkillDir skips hidden + folders without SKILL.md\\\");\\n  } else {\\n    fail(\\\"2. scanSkillDir skips hidden + folders without SKILL.md\\\", names.join(\\\",\\\"));\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// 3. migrateFromDisk: creates entries for all skills with SKILL.md mtime\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n{\\n  const skillDir = path.join(tmpDir, \\\"s3\\\");\\n  await mkdir(skillDir, { recursive: true });\\n  const ancient = new Date(\\\"2024-01-15T10:00:00Z\\\");\\n  await makeFakeSkill(skillDir, \\\"one\\\", \\\"one\\\", ancient);\\n  await makeFakeSkill(skillDir, \\\"two\\\", \\\"two\\\");\\n  const summary = await migrateFromDisk(skillDir);\\n  const all = await listAll(skillDir);\\n  const pass =\\n    summary.added.length === 2 &&\\n    summary.added.includes(\\\"one\\\") &&\\n    summary.added.includes(\\\"two\\\") &&\\n    all.skills.one &&\\n    all.skills.one.created_at === ancient.toISOString() &&\\n    all.skills.one.pinned === false &&\\n    all.skills.one.use_count === 0 &&\\n    all.skills.two.created_at.length > 10;\\n  if (pass) ok(\\\"3. migrateFromDisk seeds entries using SKILL.md mtime\\\");\\n  else fail(\\\"3. migrateFromDisk seeds entries using SKILL.md mtime\\\",\\n    JSON.stringify({ summary, skills: all.skills }));\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// 4. migrateFromDisk: idempotent — second call adds nothing\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n{\\n  const skillDir = path.join(tmpDir, \\\"s4\\\");\\n  await mkdir(skillDir, { recursive: true });\\n  await makeFakeSkill(skillDir, \\\"only\\\");\\n  await migrateFromDisk(skillDir);\\n  const summary2 = await migrateFromDisk(skillDir);\\n  if (summary2.added.length === 0 && summary2.newlyOrphaned.length === 0 &&\\n      summary2.deOrphaned.length === 0) {\\n    ok(\\\"4. migrateFromDisk idempotent on second call\\\");\\n  } else {\\n    fail(\\\"4. migrateFromDisk idempotent on second call\\\", JSON.stringify(summary2));\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// 5. migrateFromDisk: SKILL.md disappears → entry marked orphan\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n{\\n  const skillDir = path.join(tmpDir, \\\"s5\\\");\\n  await mkdir(skillDir, { recursive: true });\\n  await makeFakeSkill(skillDir, \\\"gone-soon\\\");\\n  await migrateFromDisk(skillDir);\\n  // Remove the SKILL.md\\n  await rm(path.join(skillDir, \\\"gone-soon\\\"), { recursive: true, force: true });\\n  const summary = await migrateFromDisk(skillDir);\\n  const all = await listAll(skillDir);\\n  if (summary.newlyOrphaned.includes(\\\"gone-soon\\\") &&\\n      all.skills[\\\"gone-soon\\\"].orphan === true) {\\n    ok(\\\"5. missing SKILL.md flagged as orphan\\\");\\n  } else {\\n    fail(\\\"5. missing SKILL.md flagged as orphan\\\",\\n      JSON.stringify({ summary, entry: all.skills[\\\"gone-soon\\\"] }));\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// 6. migrateFromDisk: SKILL.md reappears → orphan flag cleared\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n{\\n  const skillDir = path.join(tmpDir, \\\"s6\\\");\\n  await mkdir(skillDir, { recursive: true });\\n  await makeFakeSkill(skillDir, \\\"returns\\\");\\n  await migrateFromDisk(skillDir);\\n  await rm(path.join(skillDir, \\\"returns\\\"), { recursive: true, force: true });\\n  await migrateFromDisk(skillDir);\\n  await makeFakeSkill(skillDir, \\\"returns\\\");\\n  const summary = await migrateFromDisk(skillDir);\\n  const all = await listAll(skillDir);\\n  if (summary.deOrphaned.includes(\\\"returns\\\") &&\\n      all.skills.returns.orphan === false) {\\n    ok(\\\"6. reappearing SKILL.md clears orphan flag\\\");\\n  } else {\\n    fail(\\\"6. reappearing SKILL.md clears orphan flag\\\",\\n      JSON.stringify({ summary, entry: all.skills.returns }));\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// 7. markUsed increments use_count and records last_reason\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n{\\n  const skillDir = path.join(tmpDir, \\\"s7\\\");\\n  await mkdir(skillDir, { recursive: true });\\n  await makeFakeSkill(skillDir, \\\"alpha\\\");\\n  await migrateFromDisk(skillDir);\\n  await markUsed(skillDir, \\\"alpha\\\", \\\"first use\\\");\\n  await markUsed(skillDir, \\\"alpha\\\", \\\"second use\\\");\\n  await markUsed(skillDir, \\\"alpha\\\", \\\"third use\\\");\\n  const all = await listAll(skillDir);\\n  const e = all.skills.alpha;\\n  if (e.use_count === 3 && e.last_reason === \\\"third use\\\" &&\\n      e.last_agent_used_at !== null) {\\n    ok(\\\"7. markUsed increments count and updates reason\\\");\\n  } else {\\n    fail(\\\"7. markUsed increments count and updates reason\\\", JSON.stringify(e));\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// 8. markUsed on unknown name creates orphan entry\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n{\\n  const skillDir = path.join(tmpDir, \\\"s8\\\");\\n  await mkdir(skillDir, { recursive: true });\\n  await markUsed(skillDir, \\\"never-existed\\\", \\\"agent hallucinated a name\\\");\\n  const all = await listAll(skillDir);\\n  const e = all.skills[\\\"never-existed\\\"];\\n  if (e && e.orphan === true && e.use_count === 1) {\\n    ok(\\\"8. markUsed creates orphan entry for unknown name\\\");\\n  } else {\\n    fail(\\\"8. markUsed creates orphan entry for unknown name\\\", JSON.stringify(e));\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// 9. markListed updates last_bridge_listed_at\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n{\\n  const skillDir = path.join(tmpDir, \\\"s9\\\");\\n  await mkdir(skillDir, { recursive: true });\\n  await makeFakeSkill(skillDir, \\\"alpha\\\");\\n  await migrateFromDisk(skillDir);\\n  const beforeTime = Date.now();\\n  await markListed(skillDir, \\\"alpha\\\");\\n  const all = await listAll(skillDir);\\n  const ts = Date.parse(all.skills.alpha.last_bridge_listed_at);\\n  if (ts >= beforeTime && ts <= Date.now()) {\\n    ok(\\\"9. markListed sets last_bridge_listed_at\\\");\\n  } else {\\n    fail(\\\"9. markListed sets last_bridge_listed_at\\\",\\n      `ts=${ts} range=[${beforeTime},${Date.now()}]`);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// 10. setPinned / isPinned round-trip\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n{\\n  const skillDir = path.join(tmpDir, \\\"s10\\\");\\n  await mkdir(skillDir, { recursive: true });\\n  await makeFakeSkill(skillDir, \\\"target\\\");\\n  await migrateFromDisk(skillDir);\\n  if ((await isPinned(skillDir, \\\"target\\\")) !== false) {\\n    fail(\\\"10. isPinned default false\\\", \\\"expected false\\\");\\n  } else {\\n    await setPinned(skillDir, \\\"target\\\", true);\\n    if ((await isPinned(skillDir, \\\"target\\\")) === true) {\\n      await setPinned(skillDir, \\\"target\\\", false);\\n      if ((await isPinned(skillDir, \\\"target\\\")) === false) {\\n        ok(\\\"10. setPinned / isPinned round-trip\\\");\\n      } else {\\n        fail(\\\"10. setPinned / isPinned round-trip\\\", \\\"unpin failed\\\");\\n      }\\n    } else {\\n      fail(\\\"10. setPinned / isPinned round-trip\\\", \\\"pin set but read false\\\");\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// 11. Concurrent markUsed on same skill: no lost updates\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n{\\n  const skillDir = path.join(tmpDir, \\\"s11\\\");\\n  await mkdir(skillDir, { recursive: true });\\n  await makeFakeSkill(skillDir, \\\"hot\\\");\\n  await migrateFromDisk(skillDir);\\n  const N = 20;\\n  await Promise.all(\\n    Array.from({ length: N }, (_, i) =>\\n      markUsed(skillDir, \\\"hot\\\", `reason ${i}`),\\n    ),\\n  );\\n  const all = await listAll(skillDir);\\n  if (all.skills.hot.use_count === N) {\\n    ok(`11. ${N} concurrent markUsed calls → count=${N}`);\\n  } else {\\n    fail(`11. ${N} concurrent markUsed calls`,\\n      `expected ${N}, got ${all.skills.hot.use_count}`);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// 12. pin flag survives migrateFromDisk sweeps\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n{\\n  const skillDir = path.join(tmpDir, \\\"s12\\\");\\n  await mkdir(skillDir, { recursive: true });\\n  await makeFakeSkill(skillDir, \\\"protected\\\");\\n  await migrateFromDisk(skillDir);\\n  await setPinned(skillDir, \\\"protected\\\", true);\\n  await migrateFromDisk(skillDir);\\n  const all = await listAll(skillDir);\\n  if (all.skills.protected.pinned === true) {\\n    ok(\\\"12. pin flag survives re-migration\\\");\\n  } else {\\n    fail(\\\"12. pin flag survives re-migration\\\", JSON.stringify(all.skills.protected));\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n// Summary\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\nconst failed = results.filter((r) => !r.pass);\\nconsole.log(\\n  `\\\\n${results.length - failed.length}/${results.length} passed` +\\n    (failed.length ?   <narrative>Added required dummy TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN to check-usage-store.mjs test script to prevent config.ts initialization failures. The config module validates the presence of this required environment variable during import, causing test scripts to fail if it's missing. Following the established pattern from other test scripts, the fix uses the ||= operator to set a dummy value only when the variable is undefined or empty. This allows the test to run without requiring actual Telegram credentials, since the test exercises only usageStore functionality and never makes real API calls. The fix completes the contamination prevention work, ensuring the test script can now execute successfully with full isolation.</narrative>
